شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|WD=Q3029647}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Q210684|lt=Q210684|WD=Q210684}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == name in kana (P1814) == * おぐらじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4160005001040 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県大津市仰木4丁目38-55', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小椋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11462117}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ogura Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ogura jinja.JPG|280px|Ogura Shrine]]
Haisho |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907}}Ogi 4-38-55 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami||WD=|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami (Dark Dragon Deity)}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802}} 6 ({{ill|lt=667|Q30702|WD=Q30702}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Gosha Daigongen (Daimyōjin) / Tadosho Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ogura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Ogura Shrine''' (おぐらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. In addition to Ogura Shrine, four other shrines are enshrined here: Ōmiya Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Imamiya Shrine, and Shingū Shrine. === History === According to shrine tradition, during the transfer of the capital to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmi-kyō|1=UNKNOWN}} in Emperor {{ill|lt=Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802}} 6 ({{ill|lt=667|Q30702|WD=Q30702}}), an ascetic called Kadaifu (also written Gadaifu/Kadaifu) Sennin, who accompanied the move, invited a {{ill|lt=divided spirit|Q195793|WD=Q195793}} from {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370|WD=Q246370}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} to Ogi, marking the foundation. Later, in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), it is said to have been moved to the present site by {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Tōru|Q1284268|WD=Q1284268}}. The present site is also said to have been where Ōmiya Shrine was founded a year earlier, in {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}}), by Prince {{ill|lt=Koretaka|Q11492950|WD=Q11492950}}, son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Montoku|Q357225|WD=Q357225}}. At the former site, Takitsubo Shrine, now the {{ill|lt=okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659}}, is enshrined ({{Coord|35|06|21.9|N|135|51|13.1|E|scale:25000}}). Later, in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} 2 ({{ill|lt=902|Q23587|WD=Q23587}}), Wakamiya Shrine was enshrined. In Engi 8 ({{ill|lt=908|Q30463|WD=Q30463}}), it was listed as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. In {{ill|lt=Jōgen|Q1324833|WD=Q1324833|lt=Jōgen}} 2 ({{ill|lt=977|Q23795|WD=Q23795}}), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241}} moved to this area and stayed for ten years. In {{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736}}), Imamiya Shrine and Shingū Shrine were enshrined in the precincts. In the medieval period, it was also called '''Gosha Daigongen''' ('''Daimyōjin''') and drew devotion. In {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}} (Meiji 41), it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine name had at some point become '''Tadosho Shrine''', but in {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 20) it was restored to the current name and promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://ogurajinja.org/yuisyo.html |title=Ogura Shrine History |accessdate=2012-12-23}}. === Enshrined deity === * Principal deity - {{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami||WD=|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami (Dark Dragon Deity)}} === Precincts === * Ogura Shrine honden * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011}} * {{ill|lt=Sugawara Shrine|Q48532247|WD=Q48532247}} * Chūmon gate * Kōbunsho * Shinsensho * Haiden * Mikoshi storehouse * Ōmiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280}} - deity: {{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|WD=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Kami}} * {{ill|lt=Taga Shrine|Q11431089|WD=Q11431089}} * Tsukamoto Shrine * Shinriki {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480}} * Garden "Shinen" * Goryōsha * Koyasu Shrine * Ryūgū Shrine (Eye-Cleansing Water Shrine) * Shingū Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Imamiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōanamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310}} - deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Repository * Shrine office * Haraedo Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Art Objects === * Stone zuigaki fences, 2 locations - {{ill|lt=Shōan|Q1327427|WD=Q1327427}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1300|Q5609|WD=Q5609}}). === Festivals === * Annual festival (May 3) :: Called the '''Ogi Festival'''. Because it often rains and the route after the mikoshi procession becomes like a muddy field, it is also called the '''Muddy Field Festival'''. It includes an old-style rite commemorating {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241}}, second generation of the {{ill|lt=Seiwa Genji|Q2164991|WD=Q2164991}} who moved to Ogi in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, as well as {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157}} and processions of mikoshi from five shrines. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907}} Ogi 4-38-55 === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://ogurajinja.org Ogura Shrine] (official site) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=17 Ogura Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[category:大津市の神社]] [[category:近江国の式内社]] [[category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

link|Q11462117}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ogura Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ogura jinja.JPG|280px|Ogura Shrine]]
Haisho |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907}}Ogi 4-38-55 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami (Dark Dragon Deity)}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802}} 6 ({{ill|lt=667|Q30702|WD=Q30702}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Gosha Daigongen (Daimyōjin) / Tadosho Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ogura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Ogura Shrine''' (おぐらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. In addition to Ogura Shrine, four other shrines are enshrined here: Ōmiya Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Imamiya Shrine, and Shingū Shrine. === History === According to shrine tradition, during the transfer of the capital to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmi-kyō|1=UNKNOWN}} in Emperor {{ill|lt=Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802}} 6 ({{ill|lt=667|Q30702|WD=Q30702}}), an ascetic called Kadaifu (also written Gadaifu/Kadaifu) Sennin, who accompanied the move, invited a {{ill|lt=divided spirit|Q195793|WD=Q195793}} from {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370|WD=Q246370}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} to Ogi, marking the foundation. Later, in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), it is said to have been moved to the present site by {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Tōru|Q1284268|WD=Q1284268}}. The present site is also said to have been where Ōmiya Shrine was founded a year earlier, in {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}}), by Prince {{ill|lt=Koretaka|Q11492950|WD=Q11492950}}, son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Montoku|Q357225|WD=Q357225}}. At the former site, Takitsubo Shrine, now the {{ill|lt=okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659}}, is enshrined ({{Coord|35|06|21.9|N|135|51|13.1|E|scale:25000}}). Later, in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} 2 ({{ill|lt=902|Q23587|WD=Q23587}}), Wakamiya Shrine was enshrined. In Engi 8 ({{ill|lt=908|Q30463|WD=Q30463}}), it was listed as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. In {{ill|lt=Jōgen|Q1324833|WD=Q1324833|lt=Jōgen}} 2 ({{ill|lt=977|Q23795|WD=Q23795}}), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241}} moved to this area and stayed for ten years. In {{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736}}), Imamiya Shrine and Shingū Shrine were enshrined in the precincts. In the medieval period, it was also called '''Gosha Daigongen''' ('''Daimyōjin''') and drew devotion. In {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}} (Meiji 41), it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine name had at some point become '''Tadosho Shrine''', but in {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 20) it was restored to the current name and promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://ogurajinja.org/yuisyo.html |title=Ogura Shrine History |accessdate=2012-12-23}}. === Enshrined deity === * Principal deity - {{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami (Dark Dragon Deity)}} === Precincts === * Ogura Shrine honden * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011}} * {{ill|lt=Sugawara Shrine|Q48532247|WD=Q48532247}} * Chūmon gate * Kōbunsho * Shinsensho * Haiden * Mikoshi storehouse * Ōmiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280}} - deity: {{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|WD=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Kami}} * {{ill|lt=Taga Shrine|Q11431089|WD=Q11431089}} * Tsukamoto Shrine * Shinriki {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480}} * Garden "Shinen" * Goryōsha * Koyasu Shrine * Ryūgū Shrine (Eye-Cleansing Water Shrine) * Shingū Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Imamiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōanamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310}} - deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Repository * Shrine office * Haraedo Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Art Objects === * Stone zuigaki fences, 2 locations - {{ill|lt=Shōan|Q1327427|WD=Q1327427}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1300|Q5609|WD=Q5609}}). === Festivals === * Annual festival (May 3) :: Called the '''Ogi Festival'''. Because it often rains and the route after the mikoshi procession becomes like a muddy field, it is also called the '''Muddy Field Festival'''. It includes an old-style rite commemorating {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241}}, second generation of the {{ill|lt=Seiwa Genji|Q2164991|WD=Q2164991}} who moved to Ogi in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, as well as {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157}} and processions of mikoshi from five shrines. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907}} Ogi 4-38-55 === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://ogurajinja.org Ogura Shrine] (official site) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=17 Ogura Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[category:大津市の神社]] [[category:近江国の式内社]] [[category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

It is located in [[Ōmi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه == name in kana (P1814) == * おぐらじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4160005001040 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県大津市仰木4丁目38-55', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小椋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11462117}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ogura Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ogura jinja.JPG|280px|Ogura Shrine]]
Haisho |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907}}Ogi 4-38-55 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami (Dark Dragon Deity)}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802}} 6 ({{ill|lt=667|Q30702|WD=Q30702}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Gosha Daigongen (Daimyōjin) / Tadosho Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ogura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Ogura Shrine''' (おぐらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. In addition to Ogura Shrine, four other shrines are enshrined here: Ōmiya Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Imamiya Shrine, and Shingū Shrine. === History === According to shrine tradition, during the transfer of the capital to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmi-kyō|1=UNKNOWN}} in Emperor {{ill|lt=Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802}} 6 ({{ill|lt=667|Q30702|WD=Q30702}}), an ascetic called Kadaifu (also written Gadaifu/Kadaifu) Sennin, who accompanied the move, invited a {{ill|lt=divided spirit|Q195793|WD=Q195793}} from {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370|WD=Q246370}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} to Ogi, marking the foundation. Later, in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), it is said to have been moved to the present site by {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Tōru|Q1284268|WD=Q1284268}}. The present site is also said to have been where Ōmiya Shrine was founded a year earlier, in {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371}}), by Prince {{ill|lt=Koretaka|Q11492950|WD=Q11492950}}, son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Montoku|Q357225|WD=Q357225}}. At the former site, Takitsubo Shrine, now the {{ill|lt=okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659}}, is enshrined ({{Coord|35|06|21.9|N|135|51|13.1|E|scale:25000}}). Later, in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} 2 ({{ill|lt=902|Q23587|WD=Q23587}}), Wakamiya Shrine was enshrined. In Engi 8 ({{ill|lt=908|Q30463|WD=Q30463}}), it was listed as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. In {{ill|lt=Jōgen|Q1324833|WD=Q1324833|lt=Jōgen}} 2 ({{ill|lt=977|Q23795|WD=Q23795}}), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241}} moved to this area and stayed for ten years. In {{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736}}), Imamiya Shrine and Shingū Shrine were enshrined in the precincts. In the medieval period, it was also called '''Gosha Daigongen''' ('''Daimyōjin''') and drew devotion. In {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}} (Meiji 41), it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine name had at some point become '''Tadosho Shrine''', but in {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 20) it was restored to the current name and promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://ogurajinja.org/yuisyo.html |title=Ogura Shrine History |accessdate=2012-12-23}}. === Enshrined deity === * Principal deity - {{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami (Dark Dragon Deity)}} === Precincts === * Ogura Shrine honden * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011}} * {{ill|lt=Sugawara Shrine|Q48532247|WD=Q48532247}} * Chūmon gate * Kōbunsho * Shinsensho * Haiden * Mikoshi storehouse * Ōmiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280}} - deity: {{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|WD=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Kami}} * {{ill|lt=Taga Shrine|Q11431089|WD=Q11431089}} * Tsukamoto Shrine * Shinriki {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480}} * Garden "Shinen" * Goryōsha * Koyasu Shrine * Ryūgū Shrine (Eye-Cleansing Water Shrine) * Shingū Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Imamiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōanamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310}} - deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Repository * Shrine office * Haraedo Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Art Objects === * Stone zuigaki fences, 2 locations - {{ill|lt=Shōan|Q1327427|WD=Q1327427}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1300|Q5609|WD=Q5609}}). === Festivals === * Annual festival (May 3) :: Called the '''Ogi Festival'''. Because it often rains and the route after the mikoshi procession becomes like a muddy field, it is also called the '''Muddy Field Festival'''. It includes an old-style rite commemorating {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241}}, second generation of the {{ill|lt=Seiwa Genji|Q2164991|WD=Q2164991}} who moved to Ogi in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, as well as {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157}} and processions of mikoshi from five shrines. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907}} Ogi 4-38-55 === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://ogurajinja.org Ogura Shrine] (official site) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=17 Ogura Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[category:大津市の神社]] [[category:近江国の式内社]] [[category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

In {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine name had at some point become '''Tadosho Shrine''', but in {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20) it was restored to the current name and promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://ogurajinja.org/yuisyo.html |title=Ogura Shrine History |accessdate=2012-12-23}}. === Enshrined deity === * Principal deity - {{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami (Dark Dragon Deity)}} === Precincts === * Ogura Shrine honden * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} * {{ill|lt=Sugawara Shrine|Q48532247|WD=Q48532247|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)}} * Chūmon gate * Kōbunsho * Shinsensho * Haiden * Mikoshi storehouse * Ōmiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} - deity: {{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|WD=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Kami|1=Wakahiru-me}} * {{ill|lt=Taga Shrine|Q11431089|WD=Q11431089|1=Taga shrine}} * Tsukamoto Shrine * Shinriki {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} * Garden "Shinen" * Goryōsha * Koyasu Shrine * Ryūgū Shrine (Eye-Cleansing Water Shrine) * Shingū Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Imamiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōanamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|1=Shinmei shrines}} - deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Repository * Shrine office * Haraedo Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Art Objects === * Stone zuigaki fences, 2 locations - {{ill|lt=Shōan|Q1327427|WD=Q1327427|1=Shōan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1300|Q5609|WD=Q5609|1=1300}}). === Festivals === * Annual festival (May 3) :: Called the '''Ogi Festival'''. Because it often rains and the route after the mikoshi procession becomes like a muddy field, it is also called the '''Muddy Field Festival'''. It includes an old-style rite commemorating {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}}, second generation of the {{ill|lt=Seiwa Genji|Q2164991|WD=Q2164991|1=Seiwa Genji}} who moved to Ogi in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, as well as {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} and processions of mikoshi from five shrines. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}} Ogi 4-38-55 === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://ogurajinja.org Ogura Shrine] (official site) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=17 Ogura Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[category:大津市の神社]] [[category:近江国の式内社]] [[category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

4160005001040 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県大津市仰木4丁目38-55', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小椋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11462117}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ogura Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ogura jinja.JPG|280px|Ogura Shrine]]
Haisho |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}Ogi 4-38-55 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami (Dark Dragon Deity)}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}} 6 ({{ill|lt=667|Q30702|WD=Q30702|1=667}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Gosha Daigongen (Daimyōjin) / Tadosho Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ogura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Ogura Shrine''' (おぐらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. In addition to Ogura Shrine, four other shrines are enshrined here: Ōmiya Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Imamiya Shrine, and Shingū Shrine. === History === According to shrine tradition, during the transfer of the capital to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmi-kyō|1=UNKNOWN}} in Emperor {{ill|lt=Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}} 6 ({{ill|lt=667|Q30702|WD=Q30702|1=667}}), an ascetic called Kadaifu (also written Gadaifu/Kadaifu) Sennin, who accompanied the move, invited a {{ill|lt=divided spirit|Q195793|WD=Q195793|1=Bunrei}} from {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370|WD=Q246370|1=Niukawakami Shrine (Q246370)}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} to Ogi, marking the foundation. Later, in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it is said to have been moved to the present site by {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Tōru|Q1284268|WD=Q1284268|1=Minamoto no Tōru}}. The present site is also said to have been where Ōmiya Shrine was founded a year earlier, in {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}}), by Prince {{ill|lt=Koretaka|Q11492950|WD=Q11492950|1=Koretaka-shinnō}}, son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Montoku|Q357225|WD=Q357225|1=Emperor Montoku}}. At the former site, Takitsubo Shrine, now the {{ill|lt=okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659|1=Okumiya}}, is enshrined ({{Coord|35|06|21.9|N|135|51|13.1|E|scale:25000}}). Later, in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=902|Q23587|WD=Q23587|1=902}}), Wakamiya Shrine was enshrined. In Engi 8 ({{ill|lt=908|Q30463|WD=Q30463|1=908}}), it was listed as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. In {{ill|lt=Jōgen|Q1324833|WD=Q1324833|lt=Jōgen|1=Jōgen (Heian period)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=977|Q23795|WD=Q23795|1=977}}), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} moved to this area and stayed for ten years. In {{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736|1=1190}}), Imamiya Shrine and Shingū Shrine were enshrined in the precincts. In the medieval period, it was also called '''Gosha Daigongen''' ('''Daimyōjin''') and drew devotion. In {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine name had at some point become '''Tadosho Shrine''', but in {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20) it was restored to the current name and promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://ogurajinja.org/yuisyo.html |title=Ogura Shrine History |accessdate=2012-12-23}}. === Enshrined deity === * Principal deity - {{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami (Dark Dragon Deity)}} === Precincts === * Ogura Shrine honden * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} * {{ill|lt=Sugawara Shrine|Q48532247|WD=Q48532247|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)}} * Chūmon gate * Kōbunsho * Shinsensho * Haiden * Mikoshi storehouse * Ōmiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} - deity: {{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|WD=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Kami|1=Wakahiru-me}} * {{ill|lt=Taga Shrine|Q11431089|WD=Q11431089|1=Taga shrine}} * Tsukamoto Shrine * Shinriki {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} * Garden "Shinen" * Goryōsha * Koyasu Shrine * Ryūgū Shrine (Eye-Cleansing Water Shrine) * Shingū Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Imamiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōanamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|1=Shinmei shrines}} - deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Repository * Shrine office * Haraedo Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Art Objects === * Stone zuigaki fences, 2 locations - {{ill|lt=Shōan|Q1327427|WD=Q1327427|1=Shōan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1300|Q5609|WD=Q5609|1=1300}}). === Festivals === * Annual festival (May 3) :: Called the '''Ogi Festival'''. Because it often rains and the route after the mikoshi procession becomes like a muddy field, it is also called the '''Muddy Field Festival'''. It includes an old-style rite commemorating {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}}, second generation of the {{ill|lt=Seiwa Genji|Q2164991|WD=Q2164991|1=Seiwa Genji}} who moved to Ogi in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, as well as {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} and processions of mikoshi from five shrines. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}} Ogi 4-38-55 === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://ogurajinja.org Ogura Shrine] (official site) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=17 Ogura Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[category:大津市の神社]] [[category:近江国の式内社]] [[category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Haisho |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}Ogi 4-38-55 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami (Dark Dragon Deity)}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}} 6 ({{ill|lt=667|Q30702|WD=Q30702|1=667}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Gosha Daigongen (Daimyōjin) / Tadosho Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ogura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Ogura Shrine''' (おぐらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. In addition to Ogura Shrine, four other shrines are enshrined here: Ōmiya Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Imamiya Shrine, and Shingū Shrine. === History === According to shrine tradition, during the transfer of the capital to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmi-kyō|1=UNKNOWN}} in Emperor {{ill|lt=Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}} 6 ({{ill|lt=667|Q30702|WD=Q30702|1=667}}), an ascetic called Kadaifu (also written Gadaifu/Kadaifu) Sennin, who accompanied the move, invited a {{ill|lt=divided spirit|Q195793|WD=Q195793|1=Bunrei}} from {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370|WD=Q246370|1=Niukawakami Shrine (Q246370)}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} to Ogi, marking the foundation. Later, in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it is said to have been moved to the present site by {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Tōru|Q1284268|WD=Q1284268|1=Minamoto no Tōru}}. The present site is also said to have been where Ōmiya Shrine was founded a year earlier, in {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}}), by Prince {{ill|lt=Koretaka|Q11492950|WD=Q11492950|1=Koretaka-shinnō}}, son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Montoku|Q357225|WD=Q357225|1=Emperor Montoku}}. At the former site, Takitsubo Shrine, now the {{ill|lt=okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659|1=Okumiya}}, is enshrined ({{Coord|35|06|21.9|N|135|51|13.1|E|scale:25000}}). Later, in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=902|Q23587|WD=Q23587|1=902}}), Wakamiya Shrine was enshrined. In Engi 8 ({{ill|lt=908|Q30463|WD=Q30463|1=908}}), it was listed as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. In {{ill|lt=Jōgen|Q1324833|WD=Q1324833|lt=Jōgen|1=Jōgen (Heian period)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=977|Q23795|WD=Q23795|1=977}}), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} moved to this area and stayed for ten years. In {{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736|1=1190}}), Imamiya Shrine and Shingū Shrine were enshrined in the precincts. In the medieval period, it was also called '''Gosha Daigongen''' ('''Daimyōjin''') and drew devotion. In {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine name had at some point become '''Tadosho Shrine''', but in {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20) it was restored to the current name and promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://ogurajinja.org/yuisyo.html |title=Ogura Shrine History |accessdate=2012-12-23}}. === Enshrined deity === * Principal deity - {{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami (Dark Dragon Deity)}} === Precincts === * Ogura Shrine honden * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} * {{ill|lt=Sugawara Shrine|Q48532247|WD=Q48532247|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)}} * Chūmon gate * Kōbunsho * Shinsensho * Haiden * Mikoshi storehouse * Ōmiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} - deity: {{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|WD=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Kami|1=Wakahiru-me}} * {{ill|lt=Taga Shrine|Q11431089|WD=Q11431089|1=Taga shrine}} * Tsukamoto Shrine * Shinriki {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} * Garden "Shinen" * Goryōsha * Koyasu Shrine * Ryūgū Shrine (Eye-Cleansing Water Shrine) * Shingū Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Imamiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōanamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|1=Shinmei shrines}} - deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Repository * Shrine office * Haraedo Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Art Objects === * Stone zuigaki fences, 2 locations - {{ill|lt=Shōan|Q1327427|WD=Q1327427|1=Shōan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1300|Q5609|WD=Q5609|1=1300}}). === Festivals === * Annual festival (May 3) :: Called the '''Ogi Festival'''. Because it often rains and the route after the mikoshi procession becomes like a muddy field, it is also called the '''Muddy Field Festival'''. It includes an old-style rite commemorating {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}}, second generation of the {{ill|lt=Seiwa Genji|Q2164991|WD=Q2164991|1=Seiwa Genji}} who moved to Ogi in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, as well as {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} and processions of mikoshi from five shrines. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}} Ogi 4-38-55 === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://ogurajinja.org Ogura Shrine] (official site) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=17 Ogura Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[category:大津市の神社]] [[category:近江国の式内社]] [[category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

4160005001040 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県大津市仰木4丁目38-55', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小椋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11462117}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ogura Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ogura jinja.JPG|280px|Ogura Shrine]]
Haisho |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}Ogi 4-38-55 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami (Dark Dragon Deity)}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}} 6 ({{ill|lt=667|Q30702|WD=Q30702|1=667}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Gosha Daigongen (Daimyōjin) / Tadosho Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ogura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Ogura Shrine''' (おぐらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. In addition to Ogura Shrine, four other shrines are enshrined here: Ōmiya Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Imamiya Shrine, and Shingū Shrine. === History === According to shrine tradition, during the transfer of the capital to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmi-kyō|1=UNKNOWN}} in Emperor {{ill|lt=Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}} 6 ({{ill|lt=667|Q30702|WD=Q30702|1=667}}), an ascetic called Kadaifu (also written Gadaifu/Kadaifu) Sennin, who accompanied the move, invited a {{ill|lt=divided spirit|Q195793|WD=Q195793|1=Bunrei}} from {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370|WD=Q246370|1=Niukawakami Shrine (Q246370)}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} to Ogi, marking the foundation. Later, in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it is said to have been moved to the present site by {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Tōru|Q1284268|WD=Q1284268|1=Minamoto no Tōru}}. The present site is also said to have been where Ōmiya Shrine was founded a year earlier, in {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}}), by Prince {{ill|lt=Koretaka|Q11492950|WD=Q11492950|1=Koretaka-shinnō}}, son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Montoku|Q357225|WD=Q357225|1=Emperor Montoku}}. At the former site, Takitsubo Shrine, now the {{ill|lt=okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659|1=Okumiya}}, is enshrined ({{Coord|35|06|21.9|N|135|51|13.1|E|scale:25000}}). Later, in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=902|Q23587|WD=Q23587|1=902}}), Wakamiya Shrine was enshrined. In Engi 8 ({{ill|lt=908|Q30463|WD=Q30463|1=908}}), it was listed as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. In {{ill|lt=Jōgen|Q1324833|WD=Q1324833|lt=Jōgen|1=Jōgen (Heian period)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=977|Q23795|WD=Q23795|1=977}}), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} moved to this area and stayed for ten years. In {{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736|1=1190}}), Imamiya Shrine and Shingū Shrine were enshrined in the precincts. In the medieval period, it was also called '''Gosha Daigongen''' ('''Daimyōjin''') and drew devotion. In {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine name had at some point become '''Tadosho Shrine''', but in {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20) it was restored to the current name and promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://ogurajinja.org/yuisyo.html |title=Ogura Shrine History |accessdate=2012-12-23}}. === Enshrined deity === * Principal deity - {{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami (Dark Dragon Deity)}} === Precincts === * Ogura Shrine honden * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} * {{ill|lt=Sugawara Shrine|Q48532247|WD=Q48532247|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)}} * Chūmon gate * Kōbunsho * Shinsensho * Haiden * Mikoshi storehouse * Ōmiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} - deity: {{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|WD=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Kami|1=Wakahiru-me}} * {{ill|lt=Taga Shrine|Q11431089|WD=Q11431089|1=Taga shrine}} * Tsukamoto Shrine * Shinriki {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} * Garden "Shinen" * Goryōsha * Koyasu Shrine * Ryūgū Shrine (Eye-Cleansing Water Shrine) * Shingū Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Imamiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōanamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|1=Shinmei shrines}} - deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Repository * Shrine office * Haraedo Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Art Objects === * Stone zuigaki fences, 2 locations - {{ill|lt=Shōan|Q1327427|WD=Q1327427|1=Shōan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1300|Q5609|WD=Q5609|1=1300}}). === Festivals === * Annual festival (May 3) :: Called the '''Ogi Festival'''. Because it often rains and the route after the mikoshi procession becomes like a muddy field, it is also called the '''Muddy Field Festival'''. It includes an old-style rite commemorating {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}}, second generation of the {{ill|lt=Seiwa Genji|Q2164991|WD=Q2164991|1=Seiwa Genji}} who moved to Ogi in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, as well as {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} and processions of mikoshi from five shrines. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}} Ogi 4-38-55 === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://ogurajinja.org Ogura Shrine] (official site) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=17 Ogura Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[category:大津市の神社]] [[category:近江国の式内社]] [[category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Later, in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it is said to have been moved to the present site by {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Tōru|Q1284268|WD=Q1284268|1=Minamoto no Tōru}}. The present site is also said to have been where Ōmiya Shrine was founded a year earlier, in {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}}), by Prince {{ill|lt=Koretaka|Q11492950|WD=Q11492950|1=Koretaka-shinnō}}, son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Montoku|Q357225|WD=Q357225|1=Emperor Montoku}}. At the former site, Takitsubo Shrine, now the {{ill|lt=okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659|1=Okumiya}}, is enshrined ({{Coord|35|06|21.9|N|135|51|13.1|E|scale:25000}}). Later, in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=902|Q23587|WD=Q23587|1=902}}), Wakamiya Shrine was enshrined. In Engi 8 ({{ill|lt=908|Q30463|WD=Q30463|1=908}}), it was listed as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. In {{ill|lt=Jōgen|Q1324833|WD=Q1324833|lt=Jōgen|1=Jōgen (Heian period)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=977|Q23795|WD=Q23795|1=977}}), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} moved to this area and stayed for ten years. In {{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736|1=1190}}), Imamiya Shrine and Shingū Shrine were enshrined in the precincts. In the medieval period, it was also called '''Gosha Daigongen''' ('''Daimyōjin''') and drew devotion. In {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine name had at some point become '''Tadosho Shrine''', but in {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20) it was restored to the current name and promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://ogurajinja.org/yuisyo.html |title=Ogura Shrine History |accessdate=2012-12-23}}. === Enshrined deity === * Principal deity - {{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami (Dark Dragon Deity)}} === Precincts === * Ogura Shrine honden * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} * {{ill|lt=Sugawara Shrine|Q48532247|WD=Q48532247|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)}} * Chūmon gate * Kōbunsho * Shinsensho * Haiden * Mikoshi storehouse * Ōmiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} - deity: {{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|WD=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Kami|1=Wakahiru-me}} * {{ill|lt=Taga Shrine|Q11431089|WD=Q11431089|1=Taga shrine}} * Tsukamoto Shrine * Shinriki {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} * Garden "Shinen" * Goryōsha * Koyasu Shrine * Ryūgū Shrine (Eye-Cleansing Water Shrine) * Shingū Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Imamiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōanamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|1=Shinmei shrines}} - deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Repository * Shrine office * Haraedo Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Art Objects === * Stone zuigaki fences, 2 locations - {{ill|lt=Shōan|Q1327427|WD=Q1327427|1=Shōan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1300|Q5609|WD=Q5609|1=1300}}). === Festivals === * Annual festival (May 3) :: Called the '''Ogi Festival'''. Because it often rains and the route after the mikoshi procession becomes like a muddy field, it is also called the '''Muddy Field Festival'''. It includes an old-style rite commemorating {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}}, second generation of the {{ill|lt=Seiwa Genji|Q2164991|WD=Q2164991|1=Seiwa Genji}} who moved to Ogi in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, as well as {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} and processions of mikoshi from five shrines. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}} Ogi 4-38-55 === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://ogurajinja.org Ogura Shrine] (official site) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=17 Ogura Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[category:近江国の式内社]] [[category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

jinja.JPG|280px|Ogura Shrine]]
Haisho |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}Ogi 4-38-55 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami (Dark Dragon Deity)}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}} 6 ({{ill|lt=667|Q30702|WD=Q30702|1=667}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Gosha Daigongen (Daimyōjin) / Tadosho Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ogura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Ogura Shrine''' (おぐらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. In addition to Ogura Shrine, four other shrines are enshrined here: Ōmiya Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Imamiya Shrine, and Shingū Shrine. === History === According to shrine tradition, during the transfer of the capital to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmi-kyō|1=UNKNOWN}} in Emperor {{ill|lt=Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}} 6 ({{ill|lt=667|Q30702|WD=Q30702|1=667}}), an ascetic called Kadaifu (also written Gadaifu/Kadaifu) Sennin, who accompanied the move, invited a {{ill|lt=divided spirit|Q195793|WD=Q195793|1=Bunrei}} from {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370|WD=Q246370|1=Niukawakami Shrine (Q246370)}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} to Ogi, marking the foundation. Later, in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it is said to have been moved to the present site by {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Tōru|Q1284268|WD=Q1284268|1=Minamoto no Tōru}}. The present site is also said to have been where Ōmiya Shrine was founded a year earlier, in {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}}), by Prince {{ill|lt=Koretaka|Q11492950|WD=Q11492950|1=Koretaka-shinnō}}, son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Montoku|Q357225|WD=Q357225|1=Emperor Montoku}}. At the former site, Takitsubo Shrine, now the {{ill|lt=okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659|1=Okumiya}}, is enshrined ({{Coord|35|06|21.9|N|135|51|13.1|E|scale:25000}}). Later, in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=902|Q23587|WD=Q23587|1=902}}), Wakamiya Shrine was enshrined. In Engi 8 ({{ill|lt=908|Q30463|WD=Q30463|1=908}}), it was listed as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. In {{ill|lt=Jōgen|Q1324833|WD=Q1324833|lt=Jōgen|1=Jōgen (Heian period)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=977|Q23795|WD=Q23795|1=977}}), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} moved to this area and stayed for ten years. In {{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736|1=1190}}), Imamiya Shrine and Shingū Shrine were enshrined in the precincts. In the medieval period, it was also called '''Gosha Daigongen''' ('''Daimyōjin''') and drew devotion. In {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine name had at some point become '''Tadosho Shrine''', but in {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20) it was restored to the current name and promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://ogurajinja.org/yuisyo.html |title=Ogura Shrine History |accessdate=2012-12-23}}. === Enshrined deity === * Principal deity - {{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami (Dark Dragon Deity)}} === Precincts === * Ogura Shrine honden * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} * {{ill|lt=Sugawara Shrine|Q48532247|WD=Q48532247|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)}} * Chūmon gate * Kōbunsho * Shinsensho * Haiden * Mikoshi storehouse * Ōmiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} - deity: {{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|WD=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Kami|1=Wakahiru-me}} * {{ill|lt=Taga Shrine|Q11431089|WD=Q11431089|1=Taga shrine}} * Tsukamoto Shrine * Shinriki {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} * Garden "Shinen" * Goryōsha * Koyasu Shrine * Ryūgū Shrine (Eye-Cleansing Water Shrine) * Shingū Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Imamiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōanamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|1=Shinmei shrines}} - deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Repository * Shrine office * Haraedo Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Art Objects === * Stone zuigaki fences, 2 locations - {{ill|lt=Shōan|Q1327427|WD=Q1327427|1=Shōan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1300|Q5609|WD=Q5609|1=1300}}). === Festivals === * Annual festival (May 3) :: Called the '''Ogi Festival'''. Because it often rains and the route after the mikoshi procession becomes like a muddy field, it is also called the '''Muddy Field Festival'''. It includes an old-style rite commemorating {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}}, second generation of the {{ill|lt=Seiwa Genji|Q2164991|WD=Q2164991|1=Seiwa Genji}} who moved to Ogi in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, as well as {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} and processions of mikoshi from five shrines. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}} Ogi 4-38-55 === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://ogurajinja.org Ogura Shrine] (official site) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=17 Ogura Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[category:近江国の式内社]] [[category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]]

4160005001040 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県大津市仰木4丁目38-55', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小椋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11462117}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ogura Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ogura jinja.JPG|280px|Ogura Shrine]]
Haisho |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}Ogi 4-38-55 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami (Dark Dragon Deity)}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}} 6 ({{ill|lt=667|Q30702|WD=Q30702|1=667}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Gosha Daigongen (Daimyōjin) / Tadosho Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ogura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Ogura Shrine''' (おぐらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. In addition to Ogura Shrine, four other shrines are enshrined here: Ōmiya Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Imamiya Shrine, and Shingū Shrine. === History === According to shrine tradition, during the transfer of the capital to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmi-kyō|1=UNKNOWN}} in Emperor {{ill|lt=Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}} 6 ({{ill|lt=667|Q30702|WD=Q30702|1=667}}), an ascetic called Kadaifu (also written Gadaifu/Kadaifu) Sennin, who accompanied the move, invited a {{ill|lt=divided spirit|Q195793|WD=Q195793|1=Bunrei}} from {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370|WD=Q246370|1=Niukawakami Shrine (Q246370)}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} to Ogi, marking the foundation. Later, in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it is said to have been moved to the present site by {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Tōru|Q1284268|WD=Q1284268|1=Minamoto no Tōru}}. The present site is also said to have been where Ōmiya Shrine was founded a year earlier, in {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|WD=Q30371|1=858}}), by Prince {{ill|lt=Koretaka|Q11492950|WD=Q11492950|1=Koretaka-shinnō}}, son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Montoku|Q357225|WD=Q357225|1=Emperor Montoku}}. At the former site, Takitsubo Shrine, now the {{ill|lt=okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659|1=Okumiya}}, is enshrined ({{Coord|35|06|21.9|N|135|51|13.1|E|scale:25000}}). Later, in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=902|Q23587|WD=Q23587|1=902}}), Wakamiya Shrine was enshrined. In Engi 8 ({{ill|lt=908|Q30463|WD=Q30463|1=908}}), it was listed as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. In {{ill|lt=Jōgen|Q1324833|WD=Q1324833|lt=Jōgen|1=Jōgen (Heian period)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=977|Q23795|WD=Q23795|1=977}}), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}} moved to this area and stayed for ten years. In {{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1190|Q19736|WD=Q19736|1=1190}}), Imamiya Shrine and Shingū Shrine were enshrined in the precincts. In the medieval period, it was also called '''Gosha Daigongen''' ('''Daimyōjin''') and drew devotion. In {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine name had at some point become '''Tadosho Shrine''', but in {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20) it was restored to the current name and promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://ogurajinja.org/yuisyo.html |title=Ogura Shrine History |accessdate=2012-12-23}}. === Enshrined deity === * Principal deity - {{ill|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuraokami-no-Kami (Dark Dragon Deity)}} === Precincts === * Ogura Shrine honden * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} * {{ill|lt=Sugawara Shrine|Q48532247|WD=Q48532247|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)}} * Chūmon gate * Kōbunsho * Shinsensho * Haiden * Mikoshi storehouse * Ōmiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} - deity: {{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|WD=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Kami|1=Wakahiru-me}} * {{ill|lt=Taga Shrine|Q11431089|WD=Q11431089|1=Taga shrine}} * Tsukamoto Shrine * Shinriki {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} * Garden "Shinen" * Goryōsha * Koyasu Shrine * Ryūgū Shrine (Eye-Cleansing Water Shrine) * Shingū Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Imamiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōanamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|1=Shinmei shrines}} - deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Repository * Shrine office * Haraedo Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Art Objects === * Stone zuigaki fences, 2 locations - {{ill|lt=Shōan|Q1327427|WD=Q1327427|1=Shōan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1300|Q5609|WD=Q5609|1=1300}}). === Festivals === * Annual festival (May 3) :: Called the '''Ogi Festival'''. Because it often rains and the route after the mikoshi procession becomes like a muddy field, it is also called the '''Muddy Field Festival'''. It includes an old-style rite commemorating {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Q2531241|WD=Q2531241|1=Minamoto no Mitsunaka}}, second generation of the {{ill|lt=Seiwa Genji|Q2164991|WD=Q2164991|1=Seiwa Genji}} who moved to Ogi in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, as well as {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} and processions of mikoshi from five shrines. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Otsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}} Ogi 4-38-55 === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === External links === *[http://ogurajinja.org Ogura Shrine] (official site) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=17 Ogura Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogura Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[category:近江国の式内社]] [[category:滋賀県の旧県社]]
